meta.description: An automated test definition for a pipeline.

pipelineId.description: UUID of the pipeline item this eval tests. Also the parentId of this eval item — evals live as children of their pipeline.

description.description: Human-readable description of what scenario this eval covers and what it is asserting.

params.description: Input parameter values supplied to the pipeline run when this eval executes. Keys match the pipeline's params[].name declarations. Null if the pipeline takes no parameters.

phases.description: Per-phase assertion sets. Only phases you want to assert against need to be listed — phases omitted here are still executed but not checked.

phaseId.description: ID of the phase in the pipeline definition to assert against.

assertions.description: Ordered list of assertions evaluated against this phase's itemsProduced after the phase completes.

id.description: Stable slug identifying this assertion within the eval. Used in eval-run results to correlate assertions with outcomes. Must be unique within the phase.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this assertion is checking and why it matters.

type.description: Assertion family. count=item count comparison; type=all/any items must be an expected type; payload=expression evaluated against item payload; meta=expression evaluated against item meta; llm-judge=agent evaluates quality and returns PASS/FAIL; no-regression=compare against golden items.

op.description: count assertions only: comparison operator applied to itemsProduced.length vs value.

value.description: count assertions only: the number to compare itemsProduced.length against.

expected.description: type assertions only: item type name (string) or array of accepted type names the produced items must match.

expression.description: payload and meta assertions only: expression evaluated per item. Variable 'item' is the element. Must return a boolean. Example: "item.payload.severity in ['P1','P2','P3']".

scope.description: type, payload, and meta assertions only: 'all' (default) — assertion passes only if every item matches; 'any' — passes if at least one item matches.

agentId.description: llm-judge assertions only: UUID of the agent item used to evaluate output quality. The agent receives all produced items as context and the assertion instructions. It must call kanecta_judge_verdict('PASS' | 'FAIL', reasoning) to record its verdict.

instructions.description: llm-judge assertions only: what the judge agent should evaluate and how to decide. Be specific — vague instructions produce inconsistent verdicts. Example: 'Review the findings. Each finding must have a specific screen element referenced, a measurable pixel difference or missing element, and a severity that matches the described impact. Return FAIL if any finding is too vague to act on.'

goldenItems.description: no-regression assertions only: UUIDs of items that represent the expected/reference output. Stored in the eval definition. The runner compares actual itemsProduced against these using the matcher strategy.

matcher.description: no-regression assertions only: 'exact' — itemsProduced must contain items with identical payload to goldenItems; 'semantic' — uses embedding similarity to check that produced items cover the same concepts as golden items (uses items_search embeddings); 'subset' — every golden item concept must appear in produced items but extras are allowed.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
